title: URGING THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION TO STRENGTHEN MENSTRUAL HEALTH EDUCATION INITIATIVES AND TO ADOPT A COMPREHENSIVE MENSTRUAL CYCLE CURRICULUM.
subject: DOE
Menstrual Cycle
Menstrual Education

actions.description: Certified copies of resolutions sent, 05-12-26.
Received notice of Adoption (Sen. Com. No. 806).
Resolution adopted in final form.
Received notice of reconsideration and Senate agreement (Sen. Com. No. 795).
One Day Notice 04-30-26
Senate agrees with House amendments.  Placed on the calendar for Final Adoption on 04-30-26.
Reconsideration of action taken on 04-28-26 in disagreeing to the House amendments.
Received notice of disagreement (Sen. Com. No. 784).
Senate disagrees with House amendments.
Received from House (Hse. Com. No. 864).
Transmitted to Senate.
Adopted with Representative(s) Gedeon, Muraoka voting aye with reservations; Representative(s) Alcos, Garcia, Matsumoto, Pierick voting no (4) and  none excused (0).
Reported from FIN (Stand. Com. Rep. No. 2265-26), recommending adoption.

The committee on FIN recommend that the measure be PASSED, UNAMENDED. The votes were as follows: 14 Ayes: Representative(s) Todd, Takenouchi, Hartsfield, Hussey, Keohokapu-Lee Loy, Kitagawa, Kusch, Lee, M., Miyake, Ratcliffe, Templo, Yamashita, Gedeon, Reyes Oda; Ayes with reservations: none; 0 Noes: none; and 3 Excused: Representative(s) Morikawa, Perruso, Alcos.
Scheduled for decision making on Thursday, 04-23-26 10:00AM in conference room 308 VIA VIDEOCONFERENCE.

Report adopted; referred to the committee(s) on FIN as amended in HD 1 with Representative(s) Gedeon, Matsumoto, Muraoka, Shimizu voting aye with reservations; Representative(s) Garcia, Pierick voting no (2) and Representative(s) Alcos, Cochran, Woodson excused (3).
Reported from EDN (Stand. Com. Rep. No. 2215-26) as amended in HD 1, recommending referral to FIN.

The committee on EDN recommend that the measure be PASSED, WITH AMENDMENTS. The votes were as follows: 8 Ayes: Representative(s) Woodson, La Chica, Amato, Evslin, Garrett, Olds, Muraoka, Souza; Ayes with reservations: none;  Noes: none; and 2 Excused: Representative(s) Kapela, Kila.
Resolution scheduled to be heard by EDN on Thursday, 04-16-26 2:05PM in conference room 309 VIA VIDEOCONFERENCE.
Referred to EDN, FIN, referral sheet 28
Received from Senate (Sen. Com. No. 464) in amended form (SD 1).
Report and Resolution Adopted, as amended (SD 1).  Transmitted to House.
One Day Notice 04-09-26.
Reported from EDU (Stand. Com. Rep. No. 3596) with recommendation of adoption, as amended (SD 1).

The committee(s) on  EDU recommend(s) that the measure be PASSED, WITH AMENDMENTS.  The votes in EDU were as follows: 5 Aye(s): Senator(s) Kim, Kidani, Fukunaga, Hashimoto, DeCorte; Aye(s) with reservations: none ; 0 No(es): none; and 0 Excused: none.
Public notice requirement waived pursuant to Senate Rule 21.
The committee(s) on EDU added the measure to the public hearing scheduled on 04-08-26 1:03PM; CR 229 & Videoconference.
Referred to EDU.
Offered.
